statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 14 of 27 countries showing critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite overall averages remaining above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
While EU-wide averages remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum with petrol at 112.8 days and diesel at 101.5 days, the distribution reveals significant vulnerabilities across member states. Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types in critical status, including diesel reserves of just 30.2 days. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical levels across all fuel categories, while major economies like Germany and Italy have critical diesel reserves despite adequate petrol stocks.

Jet fuel presents the most widespread concern, with 17 of 27 countries reporting critical or warning levels. This aviation fuel shortage affects major hubs including Germany (64.7 days) and France (67.5 days), potentially impacting European air transport capacity. Only 8 countries maintain safe jet fuel reserves, with Estonia and Slovenia showing exceptionally high levels at 365 days each.

Fuel prices reflect ongoing market pressure, with Brent crude at $110.29 per barrel representing a 52.2% increase since late February's Middle East escalation. The Netherlands faces the highest petrol prices at €2.341 per liter, while Eastern European countries like Malta and Poland maintain relatively lower prices despite their stock challenges. This price-stock disconnect suggests different procurement strategies and market access across the bloc.
keyPoints: 14 of 27 EU countries have critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg most severely affected across all fuel types
Jet fuel shows the most widespread shortage, with 17 countries below safe levels and major transport hubs like Germany and France critically low
Diesel reserves are under pressure in 13 countries, including major economies Germany and Italy with critical stocks below 75 days
Brent crude oil has surged 52.2% since the February Middle East escalation to $110.29/barrel, driving EU average fuel prices above €1.80 per liter
Despite individual country vulnerabilities, EU-wide averages remain above 90-day minimums with significant variation in national reserve strategies
